stimulus: In his De materia medica, the Greek physician Dioscorides recorded an exhaustive catalog of medicinal plants, listing roughly 600 species along with their geographic origins. For generations, scholars searched for a fully illustrated copy of this influential ______blank longing was rewarded in 1499, when a richly painted manuscript surfaced in a Constantinople archive, complete with detailed botanical plates.
stem: Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?
rationale:
Choice C is the best answer. A period correctly marks the boundary between the two sentences ("For generations...treatise" and "That longing...plates").

Choice A is incorrect because a comma alone produces a comma splice between two independent clauses. Choice B is incorrect because the coordinating conjunction "and" must be preceded by a comma to join two independent clauses. Choice D is incorrect because omitting punctuation fuses the two independent clauses into a run-on.
